title: ulex parviflorus var. calycotomoides
intro: Ulex parviflorus var. calycotomoides, also known as the Scotch gorse or small-flowered furze, is a flowering plant species in the gorse family (Fabaceae). It has been used in herbal medicine for its medicinal properties, including its antiseptic and anti-inflammatory effects. The health benefits of Ulex parviflorus var. calycotomoides include its ability to aid in digestion and reduce fever, as well as its use as a treatment for respiratory issues such as bronchitis. Herbal preparations of the plant include teas and infusions, which are often used to take advantage of its medicinal properties.
